articles.title: Putin warns about unpredictable course in Middle East conflict
articles.query: (war OR conflict OR military OR sanctions)
articles.summary: President Vladimir Putin on Thursday said the consequences of the Middle East conflict are unforeseeable and could be long-term due to its widespread impact. The war is inflicting significant damage on international logistics, production, and supply chains, Putin said at the 35th Congress of the Russian Union of Industrialists and Entrepreneurs. Putin decried how different sectors, including the extraction and processing of hydrocarbons, metals, fertilizers, and other goods and commodities, have been badly affected.

articles.title: China top chipmaker has supplied chipmaking tech to Iran military , US officials say
articles.query: (war OR conflict OR military OR sanctions)
articles.summary: China's top chipmaker has supplied chipmaking tech to Iran military, US officials say A logo of Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corporation (SMIC) is seen at China International Semiconductor Expo in Shanghai, China on Oct 14, 2020. Manufacturing International Corporation (SMIC), China's largest chipmaker, has sent chipmaking tools to Iran's military, two senior Trump administration officials said on Thursday (March 26), raising questions about Beijing's stance in the month-old US-Israeli conflict with Iran. SMIC, which has been heavily sanctioned by the US government over alleged ties to the Chinese military, began sending the tools to Iran roughly a year ago and "we have no reason to believe that any of this has stopped", one of the officials said.

articles.title: Keir Starmer warns against forgetting threat posed by Russia during Iran War
articles.query: (war OR conflict OR military OR sanctions)
articles.summary: Russia during Iran War The Prime Minister said the West faced a "war on two fronts" against Iran and Russia 26 Mar 2026, 20:01 | Updated: 23h ago Share Facebook WhatsApp Email Link Copied Link CheckCircle The Prime Minister's comments came during a meeting with military partners in Finland. Picture: Getty By Thomas Layton Sir Keir Starmer has warned that Britain and its allies should not forget about the threat Russia poses to the West amid the Iran war. Speaking at Helsinkiâs presidential palace, Sir Keir said the threat posed by Russia had grown, and announced that Ukraine's navy would join military drills with Northern European nations for the first time this autumn.

articles.title: North Korea and Belarus sign treaty as Alexander Lukashenko visits Kim Jong Un in Pyongyang
articles.query: (war OR conflict OR military OR sanctions)
articles.summary: Belarusian President Aleksandr Lukashenko (centre) begins an official visit to the North Korea, where he is welcomed at the airport by Kim Tok-hun (R), First Vice Premier of the DPRK. Photo: Getty Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko. Getty Images Namita Singh ©UK Independent Today at 06:30 North Korean leader Kim Jong Un and Belarusian president Alexander Lukashenko signed a new treaty on friendship and co-operation in Pyongyang, marking what Minsk described as a shift to a “fundamentally new stage” in relations.

articles.title: From drones to rocket fuel , China and Russia are helping Iran through supply chains
articles.query: (war OR conflict OR military OR sanctions)
articles.summary: An Iran-made unmanned aerial vehicle, the Shahed-136, is displayed in a rally in Tehran, Iran, on February 11, 2026. Photo by Morteza Nikoubazl/NurPhoto) WASHINGTON—As the US-Israeli war with Iran continues, some commentators have speculated about why China and Russia appear to be keeping their distance from the conflict. Neither appears eager to intervene militarily to support Iran.
